markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' official delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, after an earlier May target, has sharpened focus on the roughly six-month window ahead and what major cultural or industry milestones could land first. Traders are tracking high-profile music drops like Drake's anticipated Iceman album, which aligns with his recent single rollout and typical release cycles, alongside broader entertainment events such as new trailers or streaming milestones that often build pre-launch buzz. Historical patterns show Rockstar's marketing ramps intensify closer to launch, while competing album campaigns and awards-season narratives frequently intersect with GTA hype cycles. Upcoming catalysts include Take-Two's May earnings call for development updates and potential trailer reveals that could shift attention or confirm timelines.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ official November 19, 2026 release date for Grand Theft Auto VI, announced after two separate delays from the original 2025 window, has locked in overwhelming trader consensus that the open-world blockbuster will miss the June 2026 cutoff. The latest postponement, confirmed in late 2025 to allow extra polish, aligns with Rockstar’s history of prioritizing quality over rushed launches and has been reinforced by steady development updates and pre-order confirmations pointing to a holiday-season rollout. While the market-implied probability of “No” sits at 99.6 percent, any realistic upset would require an unprecedented last-minute acceleration—something the studio has never done on a title of this scale. Traders are watching Take-Two’s May 2026 earnings call for the next official signal.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial, declared after jurors deadlocked on May 15, has sharply boosted trader consensus around “No Prison Time” at 78.5%. Prosecutors now have 30 days to decide on a potential fourth trial for the 2013 Jessica Mann allegation, while Weinstein, 74, continues serving time under his upheld New York conviction for assaulting Miriam Haley and his appealed 16-year California rape sentence. His documented health issues, wheelchair use, and time already served since 2020 have shifted market-implied odds toward release or minimal additional incarceration. Upcoming catalysts include any retrial announcement, sentencing on the Haley count, and appeal rulings that could reshape total effective prison exposure in this long-running case.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2025-26 NHL regular season left the Colorado Avalanche with the league's best record at 55-16-11 and the top marks in goals scored and allowed, setting them up as clear Stanley Cup favorites entering the playoffs. Their first-round sweep of the Los Angeles Kings and strong start against the Minnesota Wild have reinforced that positioning. Meanwhile, the Carolina Hurricanes have matched that momentum with an undefeated 8-0 playoff run, sweeping both the Ottawa Senators and Philadelphia Flyers to reach the Eastern Conference Final. This head-to-head dominance between the two Eastern and Western powers, combined with solid but less proven postseason showings from the Vegas Golden Knights, Montreal Canadiens, and Buffalo Sabres, has kept implied probabilities tightly bunched around the top contenders as the conference finals approach.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2025-26 NHL regular season left the Colorado Avalanche with the league's best record at 55-16-11 and the top marks in goals scored and allowed, setting them up as clear Stanley Cup favorites entering the playoffs. Their first-round sweep of the Los Angeles Kings and strong start against the Minnesota Wild have reinforced that positioning. Meanwhile, the Carolina Hurricanes have matched that momentum with an undefeated 8-0 playoff run, sweeping both the Ottawa Senators and Philadelphia Flyers to reach the Eastern Conference Final. This head-to-head dominance between the two Eastern and Western powers, combined with solid but less proven postseason showings from the Vegas Golden Knights, Montreal Canadiens, and Buffalo Sabres, has kept implied probabilities tightly bunched around the top contenders as the conference finals approach.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 64-18 regular-season mark and deep playoff run, including a sweep through the first two Western Conference rounds behind Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Chet Holmgren, have anchored their position as the leading favorite. The San Antonio Spurs' 62-20 finish and four regular-season wins over the Thunder, powered by Victor Wembanyama's All-NBA and Defensive Player of the Year caliber play, explain their strong second-place standing ahead of the Western Conference Finals matchup starting May 18. Eastern Conference contenders like the New York Knicks, Detroit Pistons, and Cleveland Cavaliers trail due to tougher paths through their bracket, though recent playoff wins keep modest implied probabilities alive for any team reaching the Finals with momentum.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France and Spain lead trader consensus for the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner due to their elite depth, recent major tournament success, and strong qualifying campaigns that showcased attacking talent like Kylian Mbappé alongside Spain’s Euro 2024-winning core of Lamine Yamal and Pedri. England, Brazil, Argentina, and Portugal follow closely in implied probabilities because of proven knockout experience, squad balance, and favorable group-stage paths in the expanded 48-team format. Recent friendlies and power rankings have kept the top five tightly clustered, with no decisive injuries or upsets shifting momentum one month out. The bunched market reflects balanced brackets designed to separate powerhouses until later rounds, where historical head-to-head trends, home-soil advantages for CONMEBOL sides, and unpredictable group dynamics create multiple realistic paths for any contender to advance deep.
